- Original payslip for the last 3 months – if the applicants receive their salary in cash this must be stated on the payslip
- Properties, if any
- For study visit:
- Official documents from the receiving institution
- Other Personal Documents
- Salary conformation from employer
- The original leave approval from employer
- Employment contract
- If retired, proof of retirement
- If freelancer, proof of freelancing work
- Family book/ Proof of address in Vietnam
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate

- Birth certificate

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
When you apply for a visa, the application will be forwarded to the Embassy of Hungary in Vietnam for processing. All cases are considered individually to see if the conditions for granting a visa are fulfilled.
This visa is solely for the purpose of short study trip and will stay no longer than 90 days
You will need to submit a copy of a travel medical insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care or emergency hospital treatment. The insurance may be bought from an insurance company in the accredited list of local insurance companies, or an insurance company with office within the Schengen area. The insurance must be valid for the entire Schengen area and cover the duration of your stay. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30.000.

## Visa Fees
Applicant's exempted from visa fee:

[Table:  Business/conference/sports event visit ]
Applicant's exempted from visa fee:
- Children under the age of 6,
- Family members of EU/EEA citizens either travelling together with or to meet their EU/EEA national family members (national marriage or birth certificate is required from the given EU/EEA country)
Service Charge:

[Table:  Business/conference/sports event visit ]
- Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
Click for the price list of Additional Services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/hun/premium-services).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee, VFS fee and other additional services fee (optional) in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only Service Charge.
- Service fee and other Additional service fee are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required

- A completed application form
- Original passport + copy of ID page, previous Schengen visas, etc.
- The passport must be valid for at least 3 months after the intended departure from the Schengen Area. Further, the document must have been issued within previous 10 years and contain 2 blank pages,
- A recent passport-size photograph not older than 6 months (4,5x3,5 cm)
- ID card of the applicant
- A round trip flight reservation
- Copy of flight ticket reservation for the whole trip (outbound and inbound flights)
- In case of visiting more than one country flight/bus/train ticket reservation
- Itinerary of the entire trip
- An original travel insurance policy
- Valid for the Schengen area and covering the entire period of intended stay. The insurance shall cover outpatient, hospital and repatriation expenses due to illness or death, with an amount of not less than €30,000.
- Proof of payment of Travel Insurance
- Hotel reservation(s) or other proof of accommodation for the whole duration of stay in Schengen; name must appear on booking
- if the inviting person provides accommodation, property ownership certificate, a copy of his/ her Hungarian address card or in case of a rental agreement : contract + the landlord’s declaration (that he/she gives permission to the tenant to accommodate guests )
- Proof of Finance
- Original bank statement for the past 3 months; Please highlight the salary transfer (if applicable)
- Sponsorship letter in case somebody else provides the funds
- Savings ; Original savings certificate
- Original payslip for the last 3 months – if the applicants receive their salary in cash this must be stated on the payslip
- Properties, if any
- For study visit:
- Official documents from the receiving institution
- Other Personal Documents
- Salary conformation from employer
- The original leave approval from employer
- Employment contract
- If retired, proof of retirement
- If freelancer, proof of freelancing work
- Family book/ Proof of address in Vietnam
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate
- In case of students : Letter from school/ University confirming their active status
- If the applicant would like to use a credit card to cover its costs: confirmation from the bank that the card is endorsed for international use and the available credit limit
- Children under 18:
- Birth certificate
- If the child is traveling alone: certified parental consent by both parents.
- If the child travels with only one parent, the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.
- Copies of the ID cards / passport of the parents
- At least one parent must attend and sign the application form , in this case the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.

## Photo Specifications
A photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
The decision about the admissible applications are to be made within 15 calendar days from the date of submission. Hence it is advised to apply earlier than 15 days before the planned departure. The consulates do not take responsibility for delayed submission and for travels failed as a consequence.
The period for decision might be extended for up to 45 calendar days

visa_types.name: Employment
visa_types.documents_required:
General requirements about applying to receive Residence permit
- Application for residence permit must be filled in with signature
- Valid passport which shall have been issued within the previous 10 years and its validity shall extend at least 3 months after the expiry of the requested visa. It shall contain at least 2 blank pages to put the visa sticker
- The applicant shall have to appear in person at VFS Global Visa Application Centre
- Fingerprints and digital photograph will be collected at VFS Global Visa Application Centre
- All copies of former passports (if any)
- All supporting documents must be submitted in origin and in copy (the original documents will be given back)
- In case of minors’ applications, a letter of approval of parents or guardian is needed
Documents to be submitted to regarding Residence permit
Employment
- Copy of ID card, birth certificate and family registration book
- Certificate of employment or letter to confirm the demand in labour resource
- Labour contract marking the salary or Pre-contract
- Documents concerning accommodation in Hungary
- Documents concerning ownership of houses in Hungary
- Certificates (if any) to prove that the applicant is qualified for the position (Legalized translation)
- Documents concerning the establishment of enterprise
- Copy of the Decision of Business registration or extract of the company issued by the Court
- Registration of the headquarter of the company attached with Rental Office contract
- Documents concerning the financial background of the company such as Tax account, manually finance report, bank balance
- Letter of confirmation of the number of employees
- Savings account of the applicant
- Travel insurance and one-way ticket (given at the Embassy when coming to collect visa)
- Other documents
The Embassy or Immigration Office may require the applicant to submit additional documents to verify the information

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
When you apply for a visa, the application will be forwarded to the Embassy of Hungary in Vietnam for processing. All cases are considered individually to see if the conditions for granting a visa are fulfilled.
The possession of a visa does not give you an automatic right of entry to a Schengen country.
Please be noted that the appointment submission will be taken with Embassy.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Employment]
- Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
Click for the price list of Additional Services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/hun/premium-services).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee, VFS fee and other additional services fee (optional) in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Service fee and other Additional service fee are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
General requirements about applying to receive Residence permit
- Application for residence permit must be fully filled out with signature
- Valid passport which shall have been issued within the previous 10 years and its validity shall extend at least 3 months after the expiry of the requested visa. It shall contain at least 2 blank pages to put the visa sticker on
In Ha Noi:  
- The applicant shall have to appear in person at the Embassy when submitting the application 
- Fingerprints and digital photograph will be collected at the Embassy 
In Ho Chi Minh City:  
- The applicant shall have to appear in person at VFS Global Visa Application Centre 
- Fingerprints and digital photograph will be collected at VFS Global Visa Application Centre 

- 1 recent passport-size photo 
- All copies of former passports (if any)
- All supporting documents must be submitted in origin and in copy (the original documents will be given back)
- In case of minors’ applications, a letter of approval of parents or guardian is needed
Documents to be submitted to regarding Residence permit
Family Reunification
- Copy of ID card, birth certificate and family registration book
- Legalized marriage certificate or any other document attesting the existence of family relationship
- Legalized birth certificate or any other document attesting the existence of family relationship
- Copies of all documents related to person residing in Hungary who will reunite (personal documents, confirmation of vacancy, especially the accommodation and financal background)
- Copy of residence permit of person residing in Hungary who will reunite. Please be noted that the validity of the residence permit must be at least 3 months when the application is submitted
The Embassy or Immigration Office may require the applicants to submit additional documents to verify the information

## Photo Specifications
A photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
The regional directorate shall rule on the application for residence permit for the purpose of family reunification within 21 days or extension if needed.

visa_types.name: Business/conference/sports event visit
visa_types.documents_required:
- A completed application form
- Original passport + copy of ID page, previous Schengen visas, etc.
- The passport must be valid for at least 3 months after the intended departure from the Schengen Area. Further, the document must have been issued within previous 10 years and contain 2 blank pages,
- A recent passport-size photograph not older than 6 months (4,5x3,5 cm)
- ID card of the applicant
- A round trip flight reservation
- Copy of flight ticket reservation for the whole trip (outbound and inbound flights)
- In case of visiting more than one country flight/bus/train ticket reservation
- Itinerary of the entire trip
- An original travel insurance policy
- Valid for the Schengen area and covering the entire period of intended stay. The insurance shall cover outpatient, hospital and repatriation expenses due to illness or death, with an amount of not less than €30,000.
- Proof of payment of Travel Insurance
- Hotel reservation(s) or other proof of accommodation for the whole duration of stay in Schengen; name must appear on booking
- if the inviting person provides accommodation, property ownership certificate, a copy of his/ her Hungarian address card or in case of a rental agreement : contract + the landlord’s declaration (that he/she gives permission to the tenant to accommodate guests )
- Proof of Finance
- Original bank statement for the past 3 months; Please highlight the salary transfer (if applicable)
- Sponsorship letter in case somebody else provides the funds
- Savings ; Original savings certificate
- Original payslip for the last 3 months – if the applicants receive their salary in cash this must be stated on the payslip
- Properties, if any
- For business/conference/sports event visit:
A letter from the inviting company/organizer and from the sending institution (if any) and be attached with:
- ID copy of the company representative / contact person
- Some kind of proof that the person can act on the Company’s behalf / they are authorize to sign for the company
- If the inviting company is responsible for the costs of the invited person, bank statement of the company for the last 3 months
- Other Personal Documents
- Salary conformation from employer
- The original leave approval from employer
- Employment contract
- If retired, proof of retirement
- If freelancer, proof of freelancing work
- Family book/ Proof of address in Vietnam
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate
- In case of students : Letter from school/ University confirming their active status
- If the applicant would like to use a credit card to cover its costs: confirmation from the bank that the card is endorsed for international use and the available credit limit
- Children under 18:
- Birth certificate
- If the child is traveling alone: certified parental consent by both parents.
- If the child travels with only one parent, the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.
- Copies of the ID cards / passport of the parents
- At least one parent must attend and sign the application form , in this case the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
When you apply for a visa, the application will be forwarded to the Embassy of Hungary in Vietnam for processing. All cases are considered individually to see if the conditions for granting a visa are fulfilled.
This visa is solely for the purpose of visiting family and friends.
You will need to submit a copy of a travel medical insurance policy that will cover any costs of repatriation on medical grounds, urgent medical care or emergency hospital treatment. The insurance may be bought from an insurance company in the accredited list of local insurance companies, or an insurance company with office within the Schengen area. The insurance must be valid for the entire Schengen area and cover the duration of your stay. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30.000.
The possession of a visa does not give you an automatic right of entry to a Schengen country.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Business/conference/sports event visit ]
Applicant's exempted from visa fee:
- Children under the age of 6,
- Family members of EU/EEA citizens either travelling together with or to meet their EU/EEA national family members (national marriage or birth certificate is required from the given EU/EEA country)
Service Charge:

[Table:  Business/conference/sports event visit ]
- Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
Click for the price list of Additional Services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/hun/premium-services).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee, VFS fee and other additional services fee (optional) in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only Service Charge.
- Service fee and other Additional service fee are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required

- A completed application form
- Original passport + copy of ID page, previous Schengen visas, etc.
- The passport must be valid for at least 3 months after the intended departure from the Schengen Area. Further, the document must have been issued within previous 10 years and contain 2 blank pages,
- A recent passport-size photograph not older than 6 months (4,5x3,5 cm)
- ID card of the applicant
- A round trip flight reservation
- Copy of flight ticket reservation for the whole trip (outbound and inbound flights)
- In case of visiting more than one country flight/bus/train ticket reservation
- Itinerary of the entire trip
- An original travel insurance policy
- Valid for the Schengen area and covering the entire period of intended stay. The insurance shall cover outpatient, hospital and repatriation expenses due to illness or death, with an amount of not less than €30,000.
- Proof of payment of Travel insurance
- Hotel reservation(s) or other proof of accommodation for the whole duration of stay in Schengen; name must appear on booking
- if the inviting person provides accomodation, property ownership certificate, a copy of his/ her Hungarian address card or in case of a rental agreement : contract + the landlord’s declaration (that he/she gives permission to the tenant to accomodate guests )
- Proof of Finance
- Original bank statement for the past 3 months; Please highlight the salary transfer (if applicable)
- Sponsorship letter in case somebody else provides the funds
- Savings ; Original savings certificate
- Original payslip for the last 3 months – if the applicants receive their salary in cash this must be stated on the payslip
- Properties, if any
- For visiting family or friends:
- Original invitation letter, stating name, address, telephone number and period of stay, their relationship + 2 wittnesses / Notarized invitation letter
- if family, proof of relationship
- ID/ passport copy of the inviting person
- if the inviting person provides accommodation, property ownership certificate, a copy of his/ her Hungarian address card or in case of a rental agreement : contract + the landlord’s declaration ( that he/she gives permission to the tenant to accommodate guests )
- Visiting family member studying in Hungary – proof of studies (active status), certificate from university or valid residence permit card
- Other Personal Documents
- Salary conformation from employer
- The original leave approval from employer
- Employment contract
- If retired, proof of retirement
- If freelancer, proof of freelancing work
- Family book/ Proof of address in Vietnam
- Birth certificate
- Marriage certificate
- In case of students : Letter from school/ University confirming their active status
- If the applicant would like to use a credit card to cover its costs: confirmation from the bank that the card is endorsed for international use and the available credit limit
- Children under 18:
- Birth certificate
- If the child is traveling alone: certified parental consent by both parents.
- If the child travels with only one parent, the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.
- Copies of the ID cards / passport of the parents
- At least one parent must attend and sign the application form , in this case the other parent must provide a notarized/certified consent.

## Photo Specifications
A photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
The decision about the admissible applications are to be made within 15 calendar days from the date of submission. Hence it is advised to apply earlier than 15 days before the planned departure. The consulates do not take responsibility for delayed submission and for travels failed as a consequence.
The period for decision might be extended for up to 45 calendar days

visa_types.name: Short Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
Competent Member State - Where to submit a visa application?
Visa applications have to be submitted at the representation of the country which is the main/sole destination of the visit (the main destination is either defined by the purpose of entry or – if the applicant plans to visit more Schengen countries with the same purpose - by the length of stay).
If visiting several states with stays of the same duration, the application should be lodged at the consulate of the the Member State whose external border the applicant intends to cross in order to enter the territory of the Member States. Thus, in line with the Schengen regulations applicants can submit the visa applications at the Hungarian representation if Hungary is the main destination.
The general rule is that an application can be lodged at the consulate which is in the country
- where the applicant resides / has a habitual residence (the applicant holds a permit that makes a stay longer than 3 months possible – e.g. residence permit),
- the applicant does not have a habitual residence or right for a long stay, but at the moment stays legally there and gave reasons for applying at that consulate.
 
This is not a personal right, it depends on the consulate’s consideration
 
and is viable for a sound reason. The stay is legal if, according to the third country’s law, the applicant is entitled to stay within the consular disctrict for a short stay (e.g. with a visa) or for a longer stay (e.g. with a residence permit).
The visa application should satisfy the following general conditions (admissibility)
- the application has been lodged
 
no more than six months and not less than 15 days before the start of the intended visit;
- the visa applicant presents a
 
legibly filled out, signed application form
. The application form may be acquired free of charge at the consulates or downloaded from the website of the Hungarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade.
- the applicant shall present a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:

- contains at least two blank pages;
- issued within the previous 10 years;
- the validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States;
- one passport-size colour photo (not older than 6 months and not edited /photoshopped)
 
is attached
- biometric data
 
have been collected (if applicable)
- visa fee is paid
*Please note that: * For applications applying for Latvia and Slovenia short stay, all documents should be translated to English. An official invitation must also be submitted regarding both countries.
Persons whose fingerprints have been collected within the previous 59 months should not have the fingerprints collected again. Applicants must indicate on the application form (field 28) when they last had their fingerprints taken.
When applying for a short stay uniform visa (Schengen C-type), the purpose of the entry can be certified with the following documents:
- Tourist visit:
 
the certificate of the travel agency; or in the case of an individual visit: the prepaid reservation of accommodation.
- Educational or other scientific or professional training purpose:
 
the certificate of the receiving institution, the certificate of the place of accommodation in Hungary
- Visiting friends, acquaintances:
 
a letter of invitation may be in the form of a public notarial document and a photocopy of the personal identification document/passport of the inviting party in Hungary.
- Family relations:
 
an invitation letter as described in the previous indent or a letter signed by the applicant in full awareness of his/her criminal liability describing the degree of the family relation, giving the name, address and phone number of the family member and the purpose of the entry and stay.
- Business:
 
in case there already exists a business partner in Hungary: a letter of invitation from the business partner, proof of the existence of commercial business relations; or the certificate of the local chamber of commerce on the existence of business relations, and the certification of the place of accommodation in Hungary.
- Medical treatment:
 
the certificate of the receiving health institution, including reference to the fact that the expected coverage of the medical treatment is available, as well as the certification of the expected place of accommodation in Hungary.
- Cultural activities:
 
agency contract with the Hungarian service provider, invitation letter issued by the host organization
- Sport:
 
proof of accreditation / participation in the sports event or contract with the Hungarian sport club, or the certificate issued by the delegating sport club / Olympic committee / Ministry of Sport, etc.
- Conference:
 
invitation letter issued by the host organization, confirmation on participation issued by the host organization, and/or a certification issued by the delegating organization; receipt of the payment of the registration fee
- Other purposes, not listed:
 
documents about the purpose of the entry and stay.
Attention! The above list of supporting documents is non-exhaustive.
Please click here to find out Appeal procedure

visa_types.name: Study
visa_types.documents_required:
General requirements about applying to receive Residence permit
Please be noted that The Embassy requires the applicants to submit documents as follows:
- Application for residence permit must be filled in with signature
- Valid passport which shall have been issued within the previous 10 years and its validity shall extend at least 3 months after the expiry of the requested visa. It shall contain at least 2 blank pages to put the visa sticker
In Ha Noi:  
- The applicant shall have to appear in person at the Embassy when submitting the application 
- Fingerprints and digital photograph will be collected at the Embassy 
In Ho Chi Minh City:  
- The applicant shall have to appear in person at VFS Global Visa Application Centre 
- Fingerprints and digital photograph will be collected at VFS Global Visa Application Centre 

- All copies of former passports (if any)
- All supporting documents must be submitted in origin and in copy (the original documents will be given back)
- In case of minors’ applications, a letter of approval of parents or guardian is needed
- All introductions of false or falsified documents can lead to judicial inquiry
Documents to be submitted to regarding Residence permit
Study
- Copy of ID card, 
- birth certificate JUST if the applicant does not have his/her own bank account
- A certificate of admission from the relevant educational institution. If the applicant obtains a scholarship then the decision made by Ministry of Education is needed
- Letter of confirmation of Course fee payment and the bank transfer receipt
- Proof of accommodation (Institution campus or rented flats): House for lease contract with the signatures both of the lessor and the lessee
- Medical insurance with the validity of 1 year /validity of 30 days+ declaration about that the applicant will be able to cover his/her medical costs in Hungary if it will be necessary
- High school certificate and University Degree or Diploma
- The sponsor or parents are responsible of submitting the confirmation of vacancy or pension book, saving book, bank statement in 3 latest months, certificate of land use right or ownership of houses, letter of sponsor for all of expenses during the applicant’s stay in Hungary (this letter must be legalized by the competent authority) OR Bank account confirmation of the applicant 
The Embassy or Immigration Office may require the applicants to submit additional documents to verify the information

visa_types.name: Family members of EU/EEA/CH nationals
visa_types.overview:
Information for family members of EU/EEA/CH nationals
The Directive 2004/38/EC (Directive of the European Parliament and of the Council of 29 April 2004 on the right of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States) extends the right to mobility within the territory of EU/EEA/CH to family members of EU/EEA/CH citizens too. Member States shall grant family members every facility to obtain the necessary visas, as long as the family member travels with or visits the EU/EEA/CH national.
Derogations from the general rules of the Visa Code applying for family members
- No visa fee can be charged.
- Family members can lodge their application directly at the consulate without any costs. Where family members decide not to make use of their right to lodge their application directly at the consulate but to use the extra services, they should pay for these services
- Third country family members of EU/EEA/CH nationals are entitled to apply for a C-EGT Schengen visa for stays of up to 90 days (in any 180-day period).
- The visas must be issued as soon as possible.
